summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 9 warning, 13 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `.team/runbooks/*.yml` — No built-in runbooks use `include:`, so users lack a working example to copy from. Backlog as docs task.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:262` — `resolveRunbookTasks` trusts referenced tasks have valid `title`. Safe in current call paths (always through `loadRunbooks`), but fragile if the function becomes a public API.
🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:267` — Nested includes are silently dropped. A `console.warn` for skipped nested includes would aid runbook authors debugging unexpected behavior.
🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:271` — No test for self-referential include (`include: "self-id"`); add one to protect the `!t.include` guard against regression
🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:271` — No test for multiple `include:` entries in a single runbook; add one to verify ordering is preserved when interspersed with normal tasks

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:110` — `isSafeRegex()` heuristic can miss some ReDoS patterns (e.g., `(\d+\.)+\d+`); consider documenting the limitation or using `safe-regex2`. Low severity — runbooks are developer-authored local files.
No 🔴 critical findings. Two 🟡 warnings for backlog. Two 🔵 suggestions.
🟡 bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:194 — `flow` field plumbed end-to-end but 0/3 shipped runbooks set it; add flow to at least one built-in or document as optional
🟡 .team/runbooks/ — No built-in runbook uses `include:`; feature is tested but 0 real-world exercisers; consider a shared-setup runbook or authoring guide

- The 🟡 warnings are about **unexercised-in-production features** (`flow` and `include:`), not unnecessary code. Both are spec'd requirements — they're complete but their only consumers are test fixtures, not shipped YAML files.
